headline: EPF Dividend Falls to 5.1% for 2025
context: The Employees Provident Fund declared a 5.1% conventional dividend for 2025, down from 5.5% in 2024, citing volatile global markets and lower returns on equity portfolios.

cards.big: 15 million EPF members see returns shrink — 5.1% barely beats 2025 inflation of 3.8%
cards.sub: Real returns of 1.3% mean retirement savings grow slower than living costs

cards.big: EPF's RM1.13 trillion in assets returned only 4.2% on overseas equity versus 7.1% in 2023
cards.sub: Global tech selloff and China slowdown hammered 38% of EPF's international portfolio

cards.big: 68% of EPF members aged 54 have less than RM50,000 saved — below minimum retirement threshold
cards.sub: Pandemic withdrawals of RM145 billion in 2020-2022 permanently scarred retirement adequacy

cards.big: EPF board includes 7 government appointees versus 2 member representatives out of 20 seats
cards.sub: Critics argue members deserve more say in how their RM1.13 trillion is invested

cards.big: The EPF dividend debate hides the real crisis — most Malaysians simply don't have enough saved to retire
Should EPF mandate a minimum 60% domestic investment ratio to support Malaysian markets?
